verification.command: pnpm exec vitest --run packages/server/src/__bugz_remote_ingress.test.ts packages/server/src/node.test.ts packages/server/src/build.test.ts -t 'method|authority|Host|scheme|adapter parity'
verification.result: 3 files passed; 17 tests passed; 95 skipped by the focused name filter
1 file and 1 real HTTP/2 reproduction passed; the temporary proof is preserved in the immediate follow-up test-only commit

results.evidence: The investigator identified the raw-method-to-Fetch identity boundary; the fixed real HTTP/2 M34 regression passed in the scoped proof run.
The investigator identified rawHeaders as the pre-normalization authority source; the real duplicate-Host rejection passed in the scoped proof run.

findings.rootCause: The Node and emitted adapters accepted a non-canonical percent-encoded authority, then exposed its URL-normalized hostname and its raw Host spelling as two different app-visible authority identities.
findings.reproduction: A real HTTP/2 peer sent :authority=%65xample.com. The handler received request.url=http://example.com/ while request.headers.get('host')=%65xample.com, and the response status was 200.
findings.disposition: Open at the audited SHA; route through the ordinary remote-ingress ledger and threat matrix, then close in live and generated Node/Vercel adapters.
